summary: Pulled a large batch of file renames and the corresponding obj_files.mk updates out of PR #766 into a smaller merge. The change was mostly mechanical: many unknown/code-address based asm and C filenames were renamed to subsystem-prefixed address names, and several fighter files were moved toward character/action naming such as ftMario_Init, ftZelda_SpecialN, ftPopo, and ftNana. The PR body explicitly notes that header files were still somewhat out of sync, so this should be treated as a build-order and naming synchronization PR rather than a complete header cleanup.
what_changed: Updated obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES points at the renamed C and asm object paths instead of stale unknown/code-address filenames.

Renamed many 100%-similarity asm files from generic names such as code_80061D70.s, db_unknown_001.s, ft_unknown_005.s, and baselib_unknown_003.s to subsystem-prefixed address labels such as ef_061D.s, db_2253.s, ft_0852.s, and hsd_3A94.s.
Split the Ice Climber source layout into separate ftPopo and ftNana paths in obj_files.mk, replacing the older ftIceClimber directory entries for Popo/Nana files.
Renamed Ice Climber asm files from fticeclimber.s and fticeclimber2_nana.s to ftpopo.s and ftnana.s.
Moved src/melee/ft/chara/ftIceClimber/fticeclimber2_nana.c to src/melee/ft/chara/ftNana/ftnana.c and adjusted its include of fticeclimber.h to include through ftPopo/fticeclimber.h.

Renamed fighter files to more descriptive character/action names: examples include ftmario.c to ftMario_Init.c, ftmario2.c to ftMario_Strings.c, ftCaptain.c to ftCaptain_Init.c, ftzelda1-5.c to ftZelda_Init and ftZelda_Special* files, and ftclink.c to ftCLink_Init.c.

Renamed several generic ft source files from full code-address names to shorter ft_XXXX-style names, including code_8007C114.c to ft_07C1.c, code_8007C630.c to ft_07C6.c, code_80081938.c to ft_0819.c, and code_80081B38.c to ft_081B.c.
Renamed lbrefract files from numbered unknown/data names to lbrefract_data.s, lbrefract1.s, lbrefract2.s, lbrefract3.s, and lbrefract3.c.
Moved db_data.s into sysdolphin/baselib naming as hsd_40FF.s according to the diff excerpt.
smart_moves: Separated the mechanical rename/build-manifest work from PR #766, reducing review surface and making the rename-only portion easier to merge.
Kept renamed files content-identical where possible; the diff shows many similarity index 100% renames, which preserves decomp matching and makes the PR auditable as a path/name change.
Synchronized obj_files.mk with the new paths in the same PR, avoiding broken object list references after file moves.
Used subsystem-prefixed address fallback names such as lb_00CE, it_266F, gm_1601, and hsd_3A94 instead of vague unknown filenames, improving searchability while avoiding unsupported semantic names.
Introduced more descriptive fighter file names where the role was evident, such as Init, Strings, and SpecialN/SpecialS/SpecialHi/SpecialLw.
decomp_lessons: Large rename batches should update the build object list atomically with file moves; stale obj_files.mk entries are a common failure mode after reorganizing decomp files.
When semantics are uncertain, subsystem plus address-range naming is preferable to unknown_N naming because it is stable, searchable, and does not overclaim behavior.
Similarity-preserving file renames are useful decomp hygiene PRs: they improve navigation without perturbing matching output.
Character source layout can be clarified by separating shared/paired fighters into character-specific directories when the build manifest is updated consistently.
Header path changes may lag behind source renames; explicitly noting that headers are still out of sync helps future contributors avoid assuming the rename pass is complete.
For fighter files with known roles, use role suffixes such as _Init, _Strings, and _SpecialN rather than numbered file names like ftzelda1.c or ftmario2.c.
naming_conventions: Unknown asm fragments were renamed from '*unknown_*' or 'code_80XXXXXX' to '<subsystem>_<address-prefix>', for example ef_061D.s, it_266F.s, if_2F72.s, db_2253.s, mn_2295.s, un_2FC9.s, and hsd_3A94.s.
Fighter source files with known character initialization roles use 'ft<Character>_Init.c', as seen with ftMario_Init.c, ftCaptain_Init.c, ftZelda_Init.c, and ftCLink_Init.c.
Fighter special-move files use 'ft<Character>_Special<Move>.c' naming, as seen with ftZelda_SpecialHi.c, ftZelda_SpecialN.c, ftZelda_SpecialLw.c, and ftZelda_SpecialS.c.
Mario string data was named ftMario_Strings.c rather than a numbered ftmario2.c file.
Ice Climbers were separated into ftPopo and ftNana paths/files, including ftpopo_init.c, ftpopo.s, ftnana.c, ftnana.s, and ftnana2.c.
Library refract files were normalized from lbrefract_001/lbrefract_002/lbrefract_003_data/lbrefract_shared_data to lbrefract1/lbrefract2/lbrefract3/lbrefract_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Most asm file changes were pure renames with similarity index 100%, indicating no instruction/data changes were intended.
Object ordering in obj_files.mk was preserved while object paths were renamed, maintaining linker order and matching constraints.
The build manifest continued to reference .s.o and .c.o outputs explicitly under $(BUILD_DIR), so renamed source/asm paths were reflected at the object-file level.
Where source files moved directories, includes were minimally adjusted rather than rewriting surrounding code; ftnana.c changed its fticeclimber.h include to ftPopo/fticeclimber.h.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body says: 'Header files are still a bit out of sync,' which is the only explicit caveat available from the provided text.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that completed the header rename synchronization mentioned in PR #773.
Search for references to fticeclimber2_nana.h after the ftNana/ftPopo directory split.
Check whether all obj_files.mk entries introduced here still match current source paths.
Look for follow-up PRs derived from #766 that handled non-rename decomp changes.
Audit whether subsystem-address fallback names such as hsd_3A94 or ft_0877 were later given semantic names.
